phase: R2-0 — REAL USDC deposit + payout rail (throwaway, isolated)
auth_structure: deposit(from, amount, commitment): the pool calls usdc.transfer(from -> pool), which invokes from.require_auth(). `from` is the deposit tx's source account, so its SOROBAN_CREDENTIALS_SOURCE_ACCOUNT (the tx envelope signature) authorizes the nested transfer — no separate auth-entry signing. payout: the pool calls usdc.transfer(pool -> merchant); the pool's own address is the `from`, authorized implicitly by the pool's invocation frame (a contract moving its own funds needs no external signature).

e2e.onboarding: friendbot XLM -> changeTrust USDC -> pathPaymentStrictReceive DEX swap -> 100.00 USDC

accounting.overpay_blocked: witness generation FAILED (value conservation) — cannot pay more than the input note

e2e.conservation: deposited $5 -> merchant received $3 + $2 = $5 -> pool nets $0; pool never pays more than deposited
e2e.note: harness printed NOT CLOSED only due to one spurious intermediate SAC balance read (spend poolDelta showed 0.00 instead of -3.00); absolute accounting (balanced=true, pool_final=0, merchant got full $5) is authoritative and confirms the rail.

cpu_fee_baseline_comparison.spend_with_usdc_payout: 61.0M cpu (vs R0 change-spend 63.8M — payout transfer adds negligible CPU over the Groth16 verify)
cpu_fee_baseline_comparison.deposit_with_usdc_pull: 16.7M cpu, 0.074 XLM (token transfer + first-time pool balance storage)
